metadata.title:
NORTH COAST
READY TO


QUIT

BUSINESS PEOPLE FAVORABLE
TO THE FRANCHISE TALK


OF A REFERENDUM.

UNION DEPOT IS LOST

FRANCHISE ADVOCATES SEE
ONLY GLOOM AND


DISASTER.

pages.raw_text:
According to L. r. HambIen, at
torney for the North Coast, that
road has terminated further ef-
forts to secure franchise in Spo.
kane.


The offer of the road to buy the
city hall is off, and with this has
gone glimmering the prospect of a
new union depot, says Mr.. Hamblen.


With between $1,500.000 and 81
100000 invested in right-of-way and
terminal grounds in this city, the
North Coast people say they will
not accept franchise with ter
minal rate and common user
clause and will first quit the field.


A movement to appeal the ques-
tion of the passage of the fran
chise to referendum vote of the
people Is being agitated by some
Of the business people today, but
the North Coast has refused to
take any active part In a movement
of this kind.


All this is due to the action of
the city council last night in pass
ing a resolution that no further
steps be taken to consider the
franchise applications of the North
Coast and the Milwaukee until
these roads consent to have the
terminal rate and common user
clause inserted.


The council followed the seem
ing preponderance of public senti-
